title: Advanced Architect
description: Master Functions, Pointers, Structs, and system memory.

questions.question: A method defined inside an overarching `class` is exactly the same structurally as a standard _____.
questions.explanation: A method is just a function that 'lives' locally inside an object container.

questions.question: Information fed into a function upon calling it `print(10)` are called _____.
questions.explanation: Arguments are dynamically passed into the defined parameters.

questions.question: Two completely separate functions can legally share the name `generate()` as long as their parameter lists are distinctly different (Overloading).
questions.explanation: Function loading allows same-named methods to coexist without compiler crash.

questions.question: What does a pointer secretly store inside its variable container?
options: The true value of a referenced item.
Nothing, it is an empty shell.
The literal physical hexadecimal memory address representing a spot in the computer's RAM.
questions.explanation: Pointers hold raw addresses. They instruct the computer exactly where to fetch data from memory.

questions.question: If you define `string* myPtr`, how do you reach into the address to change the string's actual text content to "NewText"?
questions.explanation: You MUST utilize the Dereferencing Operator (*) to unlock the memory address before rewriting the text.

questions.question: A named _____ acts like a blueprint holding bundled properties, allowing developers to create multiple unique object variables from it.
questions.explanation: Before widespread OOP classes, structs acted as customized object templates.

questions.question: A `class` is practically identical to a `struct`. What is the core difference by default?
options: Classes lock all members as 'private' by default. Structs open them as 'public'.
questions.explanation: In generic C++, both handle functions and properties identically—they just have opposite access security configurations!

questions.question: An enum translates custom English labels logically into sequenced integers behind the scenes.
questions.explanation: Yes! Monday=0, Tuesday=1, etc. Enums prevent hardcoded logic errors.

questions.question: What is the primary objective of Encapsulation (using `private` specifiers)?
options: To shrink the size of code.
To prevent uncontrolled alterations to critical class data by forcing developers to request changes safely through controlled public Methods.
To make the variables invisible to the C++ compiler.
questions.explanation: Instead of `bank.money = 0` being called randomly from outside to delete your cash, `bank.withdraw(money)` processes security logic.

questions.question: The extremely fast setup method that triggers instantly upon instantiating a new Class Object is legally called a _____.
